records.ety: [F., prop. p. p. of apercevoir to perceive.]

defs: A first view or glance, or the perception or estimation so obtained; an immediate apprehension or insight, appreciative rather than analytic.
quotes: The main object being to develop the several aperçus or insights which furnish the method of such psychology.
A series of partial and more or less disparate aperçus or outlooks; each for itself a center of experience.

defs: Hence, a brief or detached view; conspectus; sketch.
